scope_findings.claim: MAESTRO develops next-generation AI agent architectures based on large AI language models (LLMs) that are capable of interpreting instructions, generating plans, using tools, and performing actions autonomously to achieve complex real-world goals.

Hybrid neuro-symbolic planning engine that decomposes high-level goals into executable sub-task graphs with pre-conditions, post-conditions, and verification checkpoints; integrates Monte Carlo Tree Search and hierarchical task networks with LLM-driven heuristic evaluation; supports self-correction through plan monitoring and replanning from verified intermediate states.

Unified adaptive memory architecture integrating working memory (short-term structured store), episodic memory (retrieval-augmented past task episodes indexed by context similarity), and semantic memory (persistent knowledge graph connecting domain concepts, tool capabilities, and learned task patterns), with a memory controller managing long-term retention, retrieval, and forgetting to sustain coherent long-horizon agent behaviour.

External Tool and API Orchestration Layer providing a typed tool registry with capability models (input/output schemas, preconditions, postconditions, cost profiles, reliability metadata), formal tool selection and invocation policies integrated with the neuro-symbolic planning engine, standardised API adapters abstracting heterogeneous protocols (REST, GraphQL, HL7 FHIR, OPC-UA), post-invocation verification and result grounding, execution monitoring with fallback/recovery strategies, and auditable tool execution traces stored in the adaptive memory architecture.

Research explicitly addresses (a) enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimisation (Pillar 1 — neuro-symbolic planning and reasoning engine), (b) innovation in memory-augmented AI agents for robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning (Pillar 2 — adaptive memory architecture), and (c) developing advanced multi-agent frameworks for collaborative heterogeneous agents with formal task-delegation protocols and convergence guarantees (Pillar 3 — decentralised multi-agent coordination).

Effective AI agent behaviour validated through appropriate agent behaviour monitoring, plan monitoring with self-correction on execution failures, conflict resolution mechanisms in multi-agent settings, and post-invocation tool output validation.

MAESTRO incorporates qualitative and quantitative KPIs, standardised benchmarking across all demonstrators, and structured progress monitoring at 6-month integration checkpoints with measurable performance targets benchmarked against operational baselines and current state-of-the-art agent systems.

Healthcare clinical decision support sector demonstrator (Apply AI sector: healthcare): AI agent system assisting clinicians in diagnostic reasoning with HL7 FHIR API integration, specialist sub-agent coordination for imaging analysis, lab result interpretation, and treatment guideline retrieval; full provenance tracking for regulatory auditability; validated with clinical partners against real diagnostic pathways with quantitative KPIs including diagnostic accuracy, reasoning trace completeness, and tool invocation success rate benchmarked against clinician baselines.

Advanced manufacturing process optimisation sector demonstrator (Apply AI sector: advanced manufacturing): multi-agent system autonomously monitoring, diagnosing, and optimising manufacturing processes in real time with OPC-UA industrial connectors, digital twin APIs, and sensor data streams through standardised adapters with real-time validation; validated in operational factory environments with quantitative KPIs including defect rate reduction, energy savings, and multi-agent coordination latency.

Logistics and supply chain operations included as a cross-sector transfer demonstrator — coordinating logistics operations across multiple actors (warehouses, carriers, customs), with the External Tool and API Orchestration Layer managing connectors to logistics tracking systems, customs clearance APIs, and carrier scheduling interfaces — presented as demonstrating architectural portability beyond the minimum Apply AI sector requirement.
scope_findings.flag_reason: Logistics and supply chain is not one of the three specifically identified Apply AI sectors in SR-07 (healthcare, advanced manufacturing, in-vehicle autonomous driving) and has no corresponding portfolio coverage slot under CC-10. SR-07 requires demonstration in at least one of the three identified sectors, which MAESTRO satisfies through healthcare and advanced manufacturing; the logistics demonstrator is supplementary and is not explicitly excluded by any entry in scope_requirements.json or call_constraints.json. However, no Tier 2B scope element in either file explicitly confirms logistics as a required or portfolio-relevant Apply AI sector for this topic. Evaluators applying CC-10 portfolio coverage logic may question whether the logistics demonstrator strengthens or dilutes the proposal's alignment with the three specifically identified sectors. Resolution required: ensure the logistics demonstrator is framed unambiguously as supplementary evidence of cross-sector generalisability and that the application clearly distinguishes it from the two mandatory sector demonstrations required under SR-07 and CC-10.

scope_findings.claim: In-vehicle autonomous driving assessed through a targeted architectural mapping analysis (WP8) evaluating MAESTRO architecture transferability to autonomous driving settings (real-time multi-agent coordination, sensor APIs, V2X communication, verified planning under safety constraints), explicitly without a full sector demonstrator and acknowledging the absence of automotive sector partners in the consortium.
scope_findings.flag_reason: In-vehicle autonomous driving is one of the three specifically identified Apply AI sectors in SR-07 and is a named portfolio coverage sector under CC-10. MAESTRO explicitly states it does not deliver an autonomous driving demonstrator and lacks automotive sector partners. A WP8 architectural mapping analysis does not constitute a sector demonstrator. Under CC-10 portfolio coverage logic, evaluators assess whether at least one funded proposal focuses on in-vehicle autonomous driving; MAESTRO's WP8 analysis does not satisfy this requirement and may not qualify MAESTRO for the autonomous driving portfolio slot. This is not a scope violation with respect to SR-07 (which requires 'at least one' of the identified sectors, satisfied by healthcare and advanced manufacturing), but the WP8 claim creates strategic ambiguity and should be clearly positioned as a supplementary architectural analysis rather than autonomous driving sector coverage. Resolution required: confirm WP8 framing does not mislead evaluators into treating MAESTRO as the autonomous driving portfolio candidate under CC-10, and assess whether the absence of an autonomous driving demonstrator constitutes a competitive disadvantage under CC-10 portfolio scoring.

scope_findings.claim: MAESTRO allocates dedicated tasks for cohesion activities with the European Partnership on AI, Data and Robotics (ADRA) and with the GenAI4EU central Hub (CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18).

All research outputs — frameworks, models, benchmarks, the External Tool and API Orchestration Layer, and demonstrator results — will be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.

MAESTRO links to the resources offered by AI Factories and Data Labs for compute-intensive training and evaluation; demonstrator results will be validated in Testing and Experiment Facilities and disseminated via European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs).
Validation targets TRL 5 by end of project across two call-identified Apply AI sector demonstrators (healthcare and advanced manufacturing) and a cross-sector transfer demonstrator (logistics).

MAESTRO's open-source framework, External Tool and API Orchestration Layer, and standardised agent interaction protocols will lower barriers for European SMEs and startups to build on next-generation AI agent technology, strengthening the European AI ecosystem's competitiveness and reducing strategic dependency on non-European AI platforms.
scope_findings.flag_reason: No Tier 2B scope element in scope_requirements.json or call_constraints.json directly confirms that lowering barriers for European SMEs/startups, strengthening European AI ecosystem competitiveness, or reducing strategic dependency on non-European AI platforms is a required or expected outcome of topic HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02. The claim is a natural extension of result dissemination goals (SR-10 / CC-05 — sharing results with European R&D community via the AI-on-demand platform) but materially exceeds those entries by asserting SME ecosystem enablement and strategic geopolitical impact as project outcomes. Per constitutional constraint: scope boundary is defined by Tier 2B only; this outcome claim has no directly confirmatory Tier 2B scope element within this skill's declared input set (scope_requirements.json and call_constraints.json). This is flagged rather than out-of-scope because the claim may be substantiatable against Tier 2B expected_impacts.json or expected_outcomes.json (outside this skill's declared input boundary). Resolution required: confirm this outcome claim against Tier 2B expected_impacts.json before the claim can be confirmed in_scope.
